assumption_text: Kibuye Prefecture is the denominator footprint for the full estimate, with Bisesero treated as an internal hotspot rather than a separate denominator polygon.
rationale: The strongest numerator and baseline population sources are both explicitly prefecture- and commune-based.

assumption_text: Verwimp’s own approximate 3% annual growth note is accepted as the preferred March 1994 population anchor for Kibuye.
rationale: It is the only explicit near-eve-of-genocide prefectural population estimate in the source set.

assumption_text: Civilian person-months are approximated with linear attrition over 85 days rather than a daily displacement series.
rationale: No Kibuye-specific daily presence series was located, but the main death wave was concentrated early and the source set still supports a bounded approximation.

assumption_text: Unknown-status deaths are excluded strictly rather than proportionally allocated.
rationale: The prompt favors nulls over silent allocation when the source record is weak.

assumption_text: No Kibuye-specific organized-armed person-month or death estimate is imputed from national FAR/RPF totals.
rationale: Using whole-country military figures inside a subnational genocide episode would violate the denominator rules.

assumption_text: The bundle standardizes the family window to 1994-04-07 through 1994-07-19 to match the request, while preserving 1994-04-06 starts where a source explicitly uses them.
rationale: The one-day difference matters conceptually for Kigali but has limited impact on the Kibuye prefectural estimate.

assumption_text: Gikongoro is kept as a proposed episode with no full DR because its best current civilian evidence remains partly model-derived from later census structure.
rationale: This is stronger than guessing a combatant denominator, but weaker than Kibuye’s registry-style victim base.

assumption_text: The late-June Turquoise zone is recognized as a plausible separate episode, but its polygon and overlap with Kibuye/Gikongoro were not reconstructed.
rationale: Belligerent structure changed materially after 22 June, yet the current source bundle does not support a defensible quantitative re-split.
